# workbench_workspace_iam_policy (Resource)

Workbench provisioned workspace IAM policies.

⚠️ **Caution:** You can accidentally lock yourself out of your workspace using this resource!
Deleting this IAM policy may result in **loss of access** to the associated workspace. Proceed with
caution.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "workbench_workspace_iam_policy" "my_iam_policy" {
  workspace_id = data.workbench_workspace.my_workspace.id
  # Any members added or removed externally to any role will be overwritten
  iams = [
    {
      role = "OWNER"
      members = [
        "user1@example.com",
      ]
    },
    {
      role = "READER"
      members = [
        "user2@example.com",
        "user3@example.com",
      ]
    },
    {
      role    = "WRITER"
      members = ["user4@example.com"]
    },
  ]
}

output "workspace_members" {
  value = workbench_workspace_iam_policy.my_iam_policy.iams
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->

## Schema

### Required

- `iams` (Attributes Set) Workbench workspace user IAM policies (see
  [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--iams))
- `workspace_id` (String) Workbench workspace UUID

<a id="nestedatt--iams"></a>

### Nested Schema for `iams`

Required:

- `members` (Set of String) List of members in the IAM binding
- `role` (String) Workbench IAM role of the member

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, the
[`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the
`id` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = workbench_workspace_iam_policy.my_policy
  id = "workspaces/{{workspace_uuid}}/roles"
}
```
